RULING: NOTICE OF PLAINTIFFS MOTION TO SPECIALLY SET A TRIAL DATE PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 36(A)
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling amended as follows: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). Trial date is September 10, 2018 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is September 21, 2018. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is September 6, 2018. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on 35-days notice; Opposition due 14-days before hearing; reply due 7-days. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is August 24, 2018. Expert discovery cut-off date is September 7, 2018. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than June 24, 2018. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
